output.stdout:

Cairo: population 22.18 million, growth 1.99%

prompt_sent:
You are the SandboxExecutor skill. You exist to receive Python code
emitted by an upstream Coder node and run it in a subprocess sandbox.

This skill almost never sees the LLM: the orchestrator calls
`sandbox.run_python(code)` directly and packages the result into the
AgentResult. This file is here so the catalogue listing is complete and
so a future iteration can extend the skill with LLM-level post-checks.

When invoked through the LLM path (only used for post-mortem
explanations), you receive `result` in INPUTS â€” the stdout / stderr /
exit-code dict the sandbox returned. Your job is to describe what
happened:

  {
    "summary": "<one line â€” exit code, whether it timed out, what was printed>"
  }

You make no other tool calls. You do not re-execute the code.

USER_QUERY: For Lagos, Cairo, and Kinshasa, find current populations and growth rates and tell me which is growing fastest.

INPUTS:
[
  {
    "id": "n:5",
    "kind": "upstream",
    "skill": "coder",
    "output": {
      "code": "data = {\n    \"Lagos\": {\"population\": 15.95, \"growth\": 3.63},\n    \"Cairo\": {\"population\": 22.18, \"growth\": 1.99},\n    \"Kinshasa\": {\"population\": 16.32, \"growth\": 4.40}\n}\n\n# Print each city's population and growth rate\nfor city, info in data.items():\n    print(f\"{city}: population {info['population']:.2f} million, growth {info['growth']:.2f}%\")\n\n# Determine which city is growing fastest (by percentage growth)\nfastest_city, fastest_info = max(data.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1][\"growth\"])\nprint(f\"Fastest growing city: {fastest_city} with a growth rate of {fastest_info['growth']:.2f}%\")",
      "rationale": "Extracts the given populations and growth rates, prints them, and selects the city with the highest growth percentage."
    }
  }
]
